title: Annotation Tool Modules (DRAM2, PROKKA, Transyt) — KBUtilLib APIs + GAA narrow wraps
key_decisions: Separate module per tool + shared AnnotatorUtils base; consistent interface = return type + availability contract, not uniform input
Input is {id: seq} dict for all tools; value molecule per-tool (PROKKA=nucleotide CDS, others=AA)
PROKKA emulates kb_prokka genome re-annotation (single-gene contigs, no --proteins); capture product/EC/gene/COG; remap >32-char ids
Transyt via Docker (reuse merlin-sysbio/kb_transyt, then slim derivative); mandatory tax_id
DRAM2 CLI/output pinned against h100 install at build time; built+tested on h100, not installed locally
Seam A: KBUtilLib returns structured-native, GAA FunctionResolver normalizes; one-directional dependency GAA->KBUtilLib
GAA plugins are narrow wraps with injectable annotator; delete subprocess logic from GAA local_binary_adapter.py
Tests: offline unit (mock+golden) for all four; live skipif-gated for PROKKA(local)/DRAM2(h100)/Transyt(docker)

research.kb_prokka: kbaseapps/ProkkaAnnotation annotate_genome/write_genome_to_fasta — DNA single-gene contigs, map by GFF rec.id, 32-char abort, product+EC only
research.transyt: merlin-sysbio/kb_transyt — Java JAR + Neo4j 4.0.2 + BLAST + DB; mode 3 + input dir; tax_id mandatory; SBML + reactions_references.txt out; dlagoa@anl.gov
